methodology.base_rating: Division-specific Elo rating updated chronologically after each fight.
methodology.opponent_strength: Built into Elo: beating a higher-rated opponent creates a larger rating gain.
methodology.opponent_context: Opponent quality is adjusted at fight time using age, recent form, recent activity, and losing streak context.
methodology.pre_fight_context: Expected win probability uses transparent pre-fight rating adjustments for age/form, title context, and elite-resume context before Elo updates are applied.
methodology.method_multiplier: Finishes and clean decisions move ratings more than split decisions, DQs, or weird stoppages.
methodology.dominance_multiplier: Significant strike differential, knockdowns, takedowns, control time, and submission attempts adjust the size of the update.
methodology.round_dominance: Per-round stats add a separate dominance score and dampen late comeback finishes when the winner was losing the round profile.
methodology.no_decision_activity: Draws and no-contests update activity, stat totals, and dominance samples without changing Elo ratings.
methodology.result_confidence: Manual annotations and low-repeatability stat patterns dampen noisy outcomes without deleting the official result.
methodology.recent_form_adjustment: Recent wins and recent Elo movement add or remove points from the post-fight rating.
methodology.recent_outcome_adjustment: The latest result adds a small recency check, with recent finish losses penalized more than decision losses.
methodology.schedule_strength_adjustment: Recent win streaks are capped when built on weak opponent quality, while strong recent schedules and dominant wins receive credit.
methodology.recent_activity_adjustment: Fighters get a small positive bump for recent ranked activity before inactivity penalties are applied.
methodology.dominance_adjustment: Average dominance across fights becomes a bounded score adjustment, not just a hidden Elo multiplier.
methodology.finish_adjustment: A fighter's finish rate adds a small bonus, while low finish rates get a small penalty.
methodology.title_win_adjustment: Recent wins over fighters with title-lineage context add visible resume credit.
methodology.elite_resume_adjustment: Career-level elite value is calculated from peak rating, elite wins, title-lineage wins, and years spent around elite opposition, then dampened by recent decline.
methodology.quality_win_adjustment: The best win adds value based on opponent rating, with older best wins and older declining opponents dampened.
methodology.inactivity_penalty: Fighters keep rating value, but lose final-score confidence after 12 months without a fight.
methodology.legacy_penalty: High raw ratings are dampened when they are supported mostly by older peak wins rather than recent ranked activity.
methodology.current_context: A current division snapshot limits rankings to the active ranked pool and adds a smaller status prior instead of forcing contender order.
methodology.entry_gate: Low-evidence ranked entries receive a visible penalty when they lack ranked or quality wins.
methodology.top_contender_credibility: Top-five and near-top-five contenders can be penalized when recent form is not backed by current snapshot support, elite resume value, or strong recent win quality.
methodology.snapshot_order: When scores are close, active higher-snapshot contenders with a recent win can be restored above lower-snapshot fighters as a transparent tiebreaker.
methodology.title_context: Manual title-lineage context protects recent title losers, former champions, and similar cases when the pure score overreacts.
methodology.division_context: Manual division moves remove fighters from old divisions and place them in the active division before snapshot policy is applied.
methodology.rank_guard: Current elite contenders are protected by a confidence-based guard; active, proven fighters get more protection than inactive or thin-resume entries.
methodology.head_to_head: Recent direct wins can move a fighter above a close-scored opponent they beat in the same division, with a stricter one-year rule.
methodology.title_guard: The current champion is kept above contenders in that division; the required adjustment is exposed in the output.
methodology.score_confidence: Adjacent final-score gaps are labeled as virtual ties, close pairs, or clear separations so rank order is not over-read when scores are tightly packed.
